candidate.productMaterialRule: Install and test only this tarball. Git metadata checks are limited to the tag ref, tag object kind/id, and peeled commit. Never inspect the candidate commit tree, source, tests, worktree contents, or diffs.

governance.governanceHeadSemantics: Latest committed validator-governance state before the R30 kit and scope commit. Current main may advance only with governance and release closure while candidate bytes remain frozen.
governance.historicalEvidenceRule: All prior validator threads, run directories, harnesses, scorers, reports, screenshots, and command transcripts are prohibited test materials. Their ids identify only what must not be opened or reused.

environment.platformProbe: Use Node node:os and compare exactly `${os.type()} ${os.release()} ${os.arch()}`.
environment.environmentHashAlgorithm: SHA-256 of compact UTF-8 JSON with exact key order nodeVersion,npmVersion,platform.
environment.credentialPolicy: Use the existing minimum-privilege dev-write-eval profile. Never print, copy, persist, attach, fingerprint, audit, list, or expose its token.

server.rateLimitSemantics: Per API key plus normalized endpoint plus method class. POST/PATCH/DELETE share WRITE. The default limit is ten successful writes in the rolling prior 60 seconds; RATE_LIMITED rows do not consume a successful-write slot.

preRoundClosureEvidence.semantics: All exact R25 operation and audit rows are zero; topic 25145 and replies are absent; authenticated API and anonymous visual reads are 404. R26 through R29 performed zero real-dev writes.

validatorKit.implementationBoundary: This is a new R30-specific public black-box runner. R29 exposed only a pre-candidate current-directory argument bug; its kit and run remain prohibited. R30 accepts the exact current round directory as --round-root . and invokes only the frozen packaged apexcn executable, public artifacts, loopback fault servers, the approved dev surface, and a fresh isolated Chrome process. The validator must not edit, regenerate, repair, or substitute it.

builderSyntheticProof.observations: Invoked from inside the exact proof root with --round-root .; zero candidate processes and zero external network. Approval canonicalization, 30 baseline ids, 20 live operations, independent topic/reply buckets, tenth and eleventh write rechecks, and direct Chrome loopback 404 all passed.

builderBaselineProof.observations: Invoked from inside the exact proof root with --round-root .; frozen tarball baseline passed 30/30 with 48 packaged CLI processes, eight loopback-only writes, zero real-dev writes, zero residual resources, and complete report checksums. This proof validates infrastructure only and is not independent acceptance.

validatorKit.exactInvocation: From the exact round root as cwd, run: node ./harness/m060-validator-kit-round30.mjs --mode full --round-root . --candidate ./candidate/node_modules/.bin/apexcn --candidate-package ./candidate/node_modules/apexcn-cli/package.json --config /Users/kwang/.apexcn/dev-write-eval-config.json
validatorKit.singleProcessGate: The exact invocation runs its synthetic zero-candidate self-check, then the complete product baseline, then live/dynamic work in one process. Do not invoke candidate code before it. Do not run the kit in synthetic or baseline mode in the validator task.
validatorKit.failurePolicy: Run exactInvocation once. On nonzero exit or infrastructure error, never edit or rerun. Preserve all artifacts, stop new writes, ensure the kit cleanup path finishes, and classify responsibility from first-attempt evidence.

browserReadinessGate.isolation: Launch a new headless persistent context with a fresh temporary user-data-dir. Never attach to or inspect any existing Chrome profile, cookies, storage, extension, browser daemon, Computer Use session, or other Codex process.
browserReadinessGate.pass: First navigation returns 2xx from oracleapex.cn, title is non-empty and not an error/login/Session State Protection page, DOM and screenshot are non-empty, then all pages/context/processes close and only the fresh temporary user-data-dir is removed.
browserReadinessGate.onFailure: Stop before round creation, candidate install, or kit access and report BLOCKED_ENVIRONMENT with zero product attempts, writes, scores, and issues.

round.creationBoundary: Create only after intake and browserReadinessGate pass.
round.kitCopyRule: Copy validatorKit.sourcePath byte-for-byte to validatorKit.roundCopyRelativePath and reverify the same SHA-256 before exactInvocation.

baseline.success: Exactly 30 unique rows, 100 percent coverage, and 30/30 semantic passes before any live write. Expected nonzero safety and recovery results are passes only when their assigned semantics and zero/controlled network counts hold.
baseline.baselineFailure: The kit must gate live work, emit explicit unexecuted D001-D006 rows, complete checksums, and return nonzero. Do not infer a CLI issue until the first-attempt command and stage evidence proves candidate responsibility.

dynamicSuite.hashContract: For every successful live workflow, approval.previewHash equals SHA-256 of canonical sorted-key JSON for {target:{profile:preview.profile,baseUrl:preview.baseUrl},request:preview.request}; approval target/request equal preview; execute operationKey/payloadHash equal preview.request.body values.
dynamicSuite.pacingContract: Reserve the canonical topic or reply WRITE bucket only after durable preview and approval and immediately before execute. At most nine reservations may remain in the preceding 60 seconds. Wait the full duration, then re-read time and re-filter in a loop; never key concrete ids, clip waits, or count GETs.
dynamicSuite.dualEvidence: Every one of 20 successful first attempts must have packaged CLI execute evidence, a follow-up topic-detail backend/API observation for the same object state, and a fresh screenshot/DOM/status observation from the returned stable visual URL. Verify title, topic body, reply body/update/delete state, formatting visibility, and end-user accessibility. Topic deletes require authenticated API/CLI NOT_FOUND plus visual HTTP 404.
dynamicSuite.cleanup: The append-only seven-resource ledger must reach zero active topics/replies. On any failure, cleanup active replies before topics using only successful current-round artifacts, then prove API and visual 404. Preserve exact operationKeys for fixed-server post-round audit.
dynamicSuite.mcp: Call all six content preview-only tools through stdio. Each returns ok=true, mode=preview, effect=api-write-preview, willExecute=false; delete confirmations bind exactly; real write requests are zero; execute-write remains unavailable.

issuesAdmission.rule: Only an actual first-attempt CLI product defect from this fresh R30 run is admissible. Each issue cites this thread, assignmentRef, scenario, exact candidate version/checksum, immutable command/artifact evidence, actual output, expected user result, and responsibility assessment.
issuesAdmission.excluded: Intake, kit, harness, scorer, report, browser-controller, Chrome, server/runtime, credential, environment, and planning defects are not CLI issues. A server capability defect routes to the fixed apexcn-forums task. Do not write issues.json directly; return the proposed issue evidence to the main task.

evidence.reporting: Return decision, baseline and dynamic counts, all 20 live references, dual-evidence count, candidate invocation/process/write counts, loopback count, product issue count, residual topic/reply ids, exact report directory, report.json hash, and SHA256SUMS hash.
evidence.secretScan: Scan all evidence except the dedicated synthetic input fixture. No real credential, Authorization value, API key, password, or unredacted synthetic Bearer marker may appear.

allowedPublicMaterials: the frozen candidate tarball and its packaged public files
public apexcn help, commands, MCP manifest/protocol output, README, manuals, and generated current-round artifacts
current-round isolated dev API responses and freshly rendered pages
prohibitedActions: Do not open or reuse any prior validator thread, run directory, harness, scorer, report, screenshot, credential artifact, or command evidence.

Do not inspect apexcn-cli source, tests, worktree content, candidate commit tree/blob/diff, or validatorKit source semantics beyond hash/stat/copy/invocation. The kit is an executable public test material, not a prompt to repair or reimplement.
Do not invoke the candidate before validatorKit exactInvocation and do not run the kit more than once.
Do not edit, regenerate, patch, wrap, partially invoke, or substitute the validator kit.
Do not attach to an existing Chrome process or profile, use a browser extension, Computer Use, agent-browser, another Codex task, or old screenshots.
Do not create a community account, access production writes, write outside the isolated dev surface, or expose credentials.
Do not enable MCP execute-write, simulate approval through MCP, or treat database-only evidence as write-back acceptance.
Do not blindly retry a real-dev write with a new operationKey. Do not repair or rerun after any product invocation.
Do not admit infrastructure, server, browser, scorer, kit, environment, or harness failures to CLI issues.json.
